en: Because it's the 40th anniversary of Luxo Jr. as well,
we wanted to show you some things that we have
ko: 럭소 주니어의 이야기를 보여줄 것들을 준비했어.

en: that kind of tell the story of Luxo Jr.
So this is literally the story of Luxo Jr.
ko: 존 라세터가 쓴 거야, 감독이기도 하고.

en: This is written by John Lasseter, who directed it.
It's really a story about a parent and a child
and this kind of play that they have.
And here are some thumbnails of the action
that John is envisioning happening on the screen.
This technology was very slow and very primitive.
And so a lot of times we were drawing things out fully
so we could really understand
what it was gonna translate to.
Really early storyboards, if you will.
When they were first doing kind of for Luxo Jr.
the animation of the lamp,
one of the employees at Pixar actually did,
they took reference photos of this person
in the parking lot jumping over a trash can
to simulate what the potential action
of the child lamp might be.
So we made Luxo Jr., it was a fantastic short
that really got people excited
about the potential of computer animation.
